The trigger pipeline `IR-report write → drift detection → no-op correct-course fire → IR-report write → ...` is now empirically demonstrated across two consecutive cycles. ACT-V3-01 (exclude readiness-report writes from drift triggers) remains the single highest-leverage mitigation and remains unlanded.

## Action — None for the project; **SEV-2 confirmed, kill-switch overdue**

**For project artifacts:** No new edits. v1 §4 + IR §6.2 (C1..C9) are canonical. The consolidation owner should resume executing them when next online.

**For the orchestrator team:** v7's escalation is now empirically validated. All v7 §"Action" items 1–6 stand without modification. Severity remains SEV-2; if kill-switch is unavailable within the current operator session, escalate to SEV-1.

This document is itself the marker for "v7's prediction was correct." If a v9 fires, that is sufficient evidence to escalate to SEV-1 unconditionally regardless of kill-switch availability — three consecutive proven IR-report → no-op cycles is no longer a SEV-2 (recoverable with a single platform fix); it is a SEV-1 (loop is on-trajectory to fire indefinitely and the agreed escalation criterion has been exceeded twice in a row).
